query: Ursula von der Leyen early 2026 Mercosur trade deal EU application despite legal review announcement

mako.raw:
{"query":"Ursula von der Leyen early 2026 Mercosur trade deal EU application despite legal review announcement","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1626,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.whitecase.com/insight-alert/eu-provisionally-apply-eu-mercosur-interim-trade-agreement-pending-cjeu-opinion","title":"EU to provisionally apply EU–Mercosur Interim Trade Agreement pending CJEU opinion | White & Case LLP","content":"EU%20to%20provisionally%20apply%20EU%E2%80%93Mercosur%20Interim%20Trade%20Agreement%20pending%20CJEU%20opinion
On 27 February 2026, the European Commission announced it will proceed with the provisional application of the EU–Mercosur Interim Trade Agreement ("iTA"), notwithstanding the European Parliament's request for a prior opinion from the Court of Justice of the European Union ("CJEU") on the agreement's compatibility with EU law.
On 21 January 2026, the European Parliament passed a resolution requesting an opinion from the CJEU on the compatibility of the EMPA and the iTA with EU law,6 while suspending the consent procedure pending the Court's review.
The Commission is expected to publish a notice specifying the date of provisional application in the Official Journal as part of next steps.12 With Argentina's and Uruguay's ratifications on 26 February 2026, provisional application of the iTA between the EU and relevant Mercosur signatory states could begin as early as May 2026.
For many industries, this will mean a phased reduction or elimination of tariffs over a transition period of up to 18 years, the introduction of tariff-rate quotas for sensitive agricultural goods, and improved legal certainty for cross-border trade in goods and services.
The Council Decisions on the iTA (Council Decision (EU) 2026/183) and the EMPA (Council Decision (EU) 2026/185), published in the Official Journal on 27 February 2026, are available, respectively, here and here.
27800 ratifying the iTA in Argentina, published in the Boletin Oficial on 26 February 2026 (in Spanish)."},{"url":"https://www.euronews.com/my-europe/2026/02/27/commission-to-implement-contentious-mercosur-trade-deal-despite-eu-parliament-opposition","title":"Von der Leyen to implement contentious Mercosur trade deal despite MEPs' legal challenge | Euronews","content":"Commission President Ursula von der Leyen moved Friday to provisionally implement the contentious Mercosur trade deal, defying a judicial review launched by MEPs that had halted the ratification process.
Negotiated over 25 years, Mercosur would create a free-trade area spanning more than 700 million people between the EU and Latin America, including Brazil, Argentina, Paraguay and Uruguay.
Nonetheless, the Commission retained the legal option to provisionally apply the deal once one or more Mercosur countries completed their own ratification procedure.
The story has been updated with comments from the French President Emmanuel Macron and MEPs."},{"url":"https://www.dw.com/en/eu-to-provisionally-implement-controversial-mercosur-deal/a-76148877","title":"EU to 'provisionally implement' controversial Mercosur deal","content":"Von der Leyen's announcement came after Uruguay and Argentina ratified the agreement on Thursday, paving the way for its provisional application.
Brazil and Paraguay have yet to ratify the deal, but von der Leyen said they are expected to do so soon.
After 25 years of negotiations, the Mercosur deal was signed in Paraguay on January 17, with the aim of creating a free trade zone of more than 700 million people.
French President Emmanuel Macron also slammed von der Leyen's announcement."},{"url":"https://www.tibagroup.com/international-trade/agreements/eu-mercosur-agreement-2026","title":"EU–Mercosur Agreement 2026: when it enters into force and what changes for businesses - TIBA","content":"Early activation of certain trade advantages."}]}

It was **a bold move by the commission president** **on a deal that was approved** by 21 of 27 member states **but was effectively paused by a majority of MEPs in the European Parliament** who voted it to send it to the European court of justice.","score":0.7997713,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.lemonde.fr/en/economy/article/2026/02/27/eu-mercosur-trade-deal-set-to-take-effect-without-waiting-for-european-parliament-vote_6750944_19.html","title":"EU-Mercosur trade deal set to take effect without waiting for European ...","content":"European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen fast-tracked the announcement on Friday, much to France's dismay.","score":0.70263255,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.eunews.it/en/2026/02/27/von-der-leyen-approves-provisional-application-of-eu%E2%80%91mercosur-deal-saying-europe-is-shaping-its-own-future","title":"EU–Mercosur deal takes effect provisionally","content":"Ursula von der Leyen announced today (27 February) the provisional launch of the agreement between the European Union and the Mercosur countries","score":0.6768127,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-hYJApY294c","title":"Von der Leyen to implement contentious Mercosur trade deal ...","content":"Ursula von der Leyen moved Friday to provisionally implement the contentious Mercosur trade deal, defying a judicial review launched by MEPs","score":0.65173227,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.83,"request_id":"b5a8d5e4-b1b1-4918-9827-fc6d94205bc2"}
